title: The file-changed stale_conditions kind shares content_pin's exact removed defect (whole-file hash fires on any edit, not the cited region) -- never cross-examined when content_pin was removed
description: TASK-106 removed content_pin/anchor-drift specifically because it 'fires anchor-drift on ANY edit to a cited file regardless of whether the cited region changed, manufacturing false-positive drift and re-review debt.' The sibling file-changed stale_conditions kind was explicitly kept ('keep file-changed baseline_hash ... stamping') but pins the SAME whole-file SHA-256 (computeFileBytesHash) at both stamp time (block-api.ts stampDeclaredBaselines) and evaluation time (context-sdk.ts evaluateStalenessCandidates) -- so it fires on any edit anywhere in the cited file, exactly the defect content_pin was removed for. This project's binding gap-explore-surfaces-class convention requires asking 'is this an instance of a more general class' during gap investigation; that question was asked richly for content_pin's own history but never asked in the reverse direction (does file-changed share this class). Confirmed by reading TASK-106's filed text, its implementing commits (73b19bca, f330a523), the preceding forensic analysis (analysis/2026-07-08-anchor-drift-provenance.md, which never mentions file-changed), and the origin design report (analysis/2026-07-05-currency-foreclosure-shape.md, which introduces both mechanisms in the same design pass without ever comparing their false-positive risk). Live-fired instance: a 2026-07-10 currency audit found R-0030 (research, status complete) flagged stale because packages/pi-workflows/src/workflow-sdk.ts changed -- but the actual edit (TASK-111, comment-only de-jargon) never touched the cited lines (483-492,597-614,651-652).

evidence.reference: fileHashOrNull computes computeFileBytesHash(abs) -- whole-file bytes, no line-range parameter anywhere in the call chain
evaluateStalenessCandidates' file-changed branch compares computeFileBytesHash(now) against baseline_hash with zero range awareness -- identical whole-file comparison

the forensic investigation preceding TASK-106's filing analyzes content_pin's provenance/defect in depth but contains zero mentions of file-changed (confirmed via grep) -- the class question was never asked in reverse

R-0030's stale_conditions carries 7 file-changed entries at whole-file baseline_hash; 5 of its 7 sibling citations[] entries carry line ranges (e.g. workflow-sdk.ts: '483-492,597-614,651-652') that were never consulted when stamping the condition

impact: Every research item using file-changed stale_conditions is subject to false-positive staleness flags on any edit anywhere in a cited file, including comment-only or unrelated changes -- degrading trust in the currency-audit signal (a real drift and a cosmetic edit look identical) and creating re-review debt identical to what content_pin's removal was meant to eliminate.
proposed_resolution: Add an optional lines field to the file-changed stale_conditions branch (research.schema.json, additive, schema 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0, identity migration). When lines is present, stamp and evaluate using a new line-range-scoped hash (computeFileLineRangeHash) instead of the whole-file hash; when absent, keep whole-file behavior unchanged (some citations have no narrower region to scope to). State the residual limitation explicitly: this is line-number-based, not content-anchored, so an unrelated edit earlier in the file that shifts line numbers can still mis-fire -- a smaller-scope version of the same imprecision, not a total fix. Correct R-0030's own live-fired entries by adding lines from its sibling citations[] entries where available and re-stamping baseline_hash under the new algorithm, through the sanctioned write path only.
